items.title: FDA reverses course on Atara, Pierre Fabre’s twice-rejected cell therapy after Prasad’s exit
items.snippet: The FDA turned away Ebvallo in January, taking issue with the design of the registrational trial. In a recent meeting, however, the agency agreed that the study could in fact support the cell therapy’s approval. The news comes a week after the departure of controversial biologics Director Vinay Prasad.

items.title: Angelini inks $4.1B Catalyst buy to enter US rare disease market
items.snippet: Catalyst Pharmaceuticals comes with a trio of approved drugs as Angelini Pharma expands its neuroscience offerings.

items.title: Gilead wipes out most of Arcellx workforce
items.snippet: Gilead is laying off Arcellx employees in California and Maryland, with some cuts effective this year and the remainder happening in 2027.

items.title: Entrada crashes as Duchenne therapy comes in ‘below expectations’ in early study
items.snippet: Entrada Therapeutics saw a 2.36% dystrophin increase in patients treated with its oligonucleotide asset—a number that fell short of the company’s prior guidance of a double-digit improvement.

items.title: Sanofi requests removal of Tzield from CNPV program after Høeg gets involved
items.snippet: Acting Center for Drug Evaluation and Research Director Tracy Beth Høeg reportedly disagreed with staff who wanted to approve Sanofi’s type 1 diabetes drug. It’s far from the first time a political appointee has allegedly meddled in a recent FDA decision.

items.title: Sarepta says to ‘exercise prudence’ in setting expectations for Elevidys’ recovery
items.snippet: Sarepta Therapeutics has put in place several initiatives to help its gene therapy Elevidys return to growth, but recovery will take a long time, according to company executives.

items.title: GSK goes beyond weight-loss with $1B buyout of Chinese siRNA specialist
items.snippet: With Siran Biotechnology under its fold, GSK will have access to a long-acting siRNA therapy that could induce weight loss while preserving lean mass, in addition to addressing other weight-related comorbidities.

items.title: Fibrosis, IPF and the search for better therapies
items.snippet: In this episode of Denatured, you'll be hearing from Georg Vo Beiske, CEO of Tribune Therapeutics and Jonas Hallén, co-founder and Chief Medical Officer of Calluna Pharma. We dive into IPF and fibrosis challenges, unpacking treatment hurdles, emerging targets, unmet needs and expansion paths beyond the lung.

items.title: Increased capital boosts biotech R&D job postings, employment
items.snippet: Biotech R&D job postings live on BioSpace rose for the fourth straight month in April, and a CBRE report shared similar findings for employment. The head of CBRE’s U.S. life sciences practice discusses what’s behind the growth and the current job market.

items.title: Winning the room: how to ace panel interviews
items.snippet: Panel interviews can play a major role in getting jobs. Two career coaches discuss what to do before and during the interview, including identifying how to differentiate yourself, engaging in true conversations and not overlooking a key panel member.

items.title: The top 12 companies hiring in biopharma now
items.snippet: Looking for a biopharma job? Check out the BioSpace list of 12 top companies hiring life sciences professionals like you.

items.title: 3 top FDA, CDC roles filled by 15 different people in less than 18 months
items.snippet: The Department of Health and Human Services is spinning its wheels, unable to establish steady leadership at three major divisions—the CDC and the FDA’s two primary review units.

items.title: Amgen, Sanofi commit nearly $600M to North American expansions
items.snippet: Sanofi’s investment will support a Canadian site’s efforts to apply AI to drug production, while Amgen has unveiled the second expansion of its Puerto Rico plant in quick succession.

items.title: Unforced errors flummox HHS’ radical transparency bid—again
items.snippet: Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s health department has consistently touted radical transparency as being key to its mission. Recent instances—the FDA’s decision not to disclose the recipients of three Commissioner’s National Priority Vouchers and FDA and CDC choices not to publish vaccine-related papers—call this intent into q...

items.title: Novo’s Wegovy pill reaches 1M patients, shattering expectations with $355M in sales
items.snippet: Analysts and investors alike had been eagerly awaiting sales figures for Novo Nordisk’s Wegovy pill. The answer blew past expectations by 86%.

items.title: CellCentric secures $220M in Europe’s biggest private biotech fundraise of 2026
items.snippet: Pfizer-backed cancer company CellCentric will use the cash to support the launch of a pivotal myeloma trial testing its potentially first-in-class oral treatment this year.

items.title: Bayer ends multi-year M&A drought with up to $2.45B Perfuse buy
items.snippet: For $300 million upfront, Bayer is purchasing Perfuse Therapeutics to advance an eye implant for glaucoma and diabetic retinopathy, marking the company’s first pharma acquisition since 2021.

items.title: Gilead guts Arcellx team as BioNTech plant closures will wipe out 1,860 jobs
items.snippet: Follow along as BioSpace tracks job cuts and restructuring initiatives.

items.title: Viridian turns green again as new Phase 3 data vindicate thyroid eye disease drug
items.snippet: Viridian Therapeutics’ elegrobart normalized the degree of eye protrusion and improved double vision in a Phase 3 study. The company plans to file for approval in the first quarter of 2027.

items.title: J&J pushes dual-antibody IBD therapy into Phase 3 despite mid-stage fails
items.snippet: Johnson & Johnson plans to advance the co-antibody therapy, which combines its IL-23 blocker Tremfya and the TNF-alpha inhibitor Simponi, into late-stage testing for Crohn’s disease and ulcerative colitis.

items.title: 3 top FDA, CDC roles filled by 15 different people in less than 18 months
items.snippet: The Department of Health and Human Services is spinning its wheels, unable to establish steady leadership at three major divisions—the CDC and the FDA’s two primary review units.

items.title: Unforced errors flummox HHS’ radical transparency bid—again
items.snippet: Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s health department has consistently touted radical transparency as being key to its mission. Recent instances—the FDA’s decision not to disclose the recipients of three Commissioner’s National Priority Vouchers and FDA and CDC choices not to publish vaccine-related papers—call this intent into q...

items.title: BioSpace elevates journalism as biopharma evolves in the Trump era
items.snippet: Neal and Azbee awards have validated our approach to reporting on the industry at a time of unprecedented shifts at the FDA and other federal agencies.

items.title: Biotech has become a good news story. Let the sun shine
items.snippet: Doubling survival in pancreatic cancer, a long-fought rare disease approval, a massive IPO and ambitious biotech entrepreneurs have BioSpace Senior Editor Annalee Armstrong feeling upbeat about the biotech scene.
